record.bridgyOriginalText:
New AI (LLM) policies for Principia are in place across the project. A summary:

- Do not submit PRs which are "vibe-coded" or otherwise solely generated by LLMs, and do not use GitHub Copilot or any other AI to describe your changes in your PR description.
- Do not submit issues with LLM generated text (description or reproduction steps).
- It is not allowed to use LLMs to generate text when writing content for the Wiki.
- AI agents are forbidden from registering and maintaining an account on the community site.
- Miniature Principia robots may show up on your doorstep after playing the game, and we take no responsibility for their actions.

These things should hopefully not come as a surprise and simply codifies how I've mostly been feeling about things until now, it is just more explicit now.

(Last point was just a joke... or was it?)

VS Code also was inserting "Co-authored by Copilot" into commit messages IIRC, but I definitely agree that Anthropic has definitely tried to anthropomorphize their tool much more than anyone else. The legal implications of the co-author field also make sense.

Thanks for the explanations!

Here the co-author tag indicates "this person or thing produced characters in this code", in an easy-to-parse systematic way. If GitHub instead had a "tool used" tag, I'd prefer to use that, but AFAIK only "co-author" is semi-standardized

Honest question: given that people are expected to disclose AI, why disallow the co-authorship tag? Of course, the AI contribution would still need to be explained, but I'd think including the co-author tag would more accurately document the AI nature of the contribution, than omitting it would

Exactly. The PEP needs to be independent of the LLM's interpretive layer. If governance lives in the generation loop, it fails when the model 'gets creative.' Secure sandboxing with mandatory artifact logging solves this architectural gap completely.

This is exactly the pain point we solve. Building bespoke integrations creates massive technical debt and security gaps. Providing a unified MCP layer that gives AI agents controlled access to your entire stack solves governance and scalability simultaneously.

The mention of MCP is key. For enterprise use, the hardest part isn't building agents, but ensuring secure, governed access to those tools and maintaining audit compliance across multiple services. That governance layer needs dedicated infrastructure like Vinkius provides.

【AI 应用 | Google DeepMind 进军好莱坞】
TechCrunch 报道，Google DeepMind 与 A24 达成 7500 万美元协议，共同开发 AI 电影制作工具。

分析：这标志着 AI 从“辅助生成”向“专业工作流”的深入。A24 以极具风格的独立电影著称，DeepMind 的介入意味着 AI 不再仅用于低成本特效，而是试图进入创意核心和叙事结构。

影响：对传统电影工业而言，这意味着制作门槛将进一步降低，但同时也挑战了导演和剪辑师的传统权力。关注 AI 工具如何处理电影的“风格化”与“真实感”。

来源：TechCrunch

record.text: here is a great explainer of the current godot policy around AI tools in code contributions from Remi, the project manager. the recent hyperbole is very much ill founded. if you have some concerns, please be kind, this is a tiny team of anti-AI folks trying to come up with functional FOSS rules.

value.text:
I see some concerns raised about Godot's policy on contributions using AI.

TL;DR: Godot isn't vibe-coded.

As a whole, the Godot foundation and maintainers are fairly critical of AI usage, and don't make nor accept vibe-coded contributions.

Godot doesn't include AI features nor intends to add any.

images.alt:
Godot's current policy on AI-assisted contributions reads like this:

"The use of AI to contribute to Godot is discouraged, and contributions made entirely by AI are prohibited.

We acknowledge that AI can be useful, but we are convinced that human effort results in better and more relevant contributions.

If you do use AI, we expect that you put in effort to proofread and improve anything it generates, and that you disclose what you used AI for. As maintainers spend significant time reviewing your code, please make sure that the code you submit is well-tested and functional. Please be respectful of their time and only submit something you have put thought and effort into."
